Title: 14 families keep Pulau Raya's 200-year heritage alive
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Pulau Raya, one of Kelantan's smallest villages, is home to 14 Chinese families preserving a way of life that has endured for more than two centuries.
Village head Wee Thin Kim, 51, said the island, believed to be more than 200 years old, had changed significantly over the decades.
He said Pulau Raya was once home to both Malay and Chinese families, but today only the Chinese community remains.
"I became the village head in 2018. Although we are a small community, we are determined to preserve the island's history and traditions for future generations," he said.
Wee said the village has only 14 houses, including six traditional homes, many of which are built using bamboo and timber.
[[nid:1492363]]
"Some residents continue to maintain these traditional houses because they are part of our heritage.
"We hope the younger generation will appreciate and preserve them," he said.
Despite its long history, Pulau Raya has no public facilities such as a school or bank.
"Most residents earn a living as fruit farmers, cultivating durian orchards, rubber smallholdings and other crops.
"Their produce is transported to nearby Tanah Merah, about a 10-minute drive away via the bridge connecting the island to Kampung Belimbing," he said.
For businessman Wee Iu Kee, 41, Pulau Raya is more than just a place to live.
The father of four said his family had lived on the island for generations and still occupied the ancestral home built by his forefathers.
"This is my family's inherited home. My great-grandfather settled here many years ago, and we have remained on the island ever since," he said.
He said his late father and ancestors devoted their lives to farming, and the family continues to care for about 50 durian trees planted by previous generations.
"My children are growing up surrounded by the same environment that shaped our family.
"I hope they will continue to value our roots and keep Pulau Raya's history alive," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samsung cuts US jobs, offers relocations ahead of HQ move
Article:
SEOUL: Samsung Electronics has cut jobs at its US display, phone and other consumer electronics operations — affecting workers mainly in New Jersey and Texas, according to documents and two people familiar with the matter.
The South Korean tech giant said on Sunday in a statement to Reuters that 739 roles in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, have been affected by plans by Samsung Electronics America (SEA) — which is focused on consumer electronics and does not include chips — to move its headquarters to Texas.
A majority of people affected have received relocation offers, but others were let go, it added without elaborating.
At SEA's Plano, Texas office, some 100 workers including staff in its mobile division, have been let go, according to one person who said they were among the employees laid off. Sources declined to be identified because of the sensitivity of the issue.
The cuts — though related to the shift in headquarters — underscore diverging fortunes within the South Korean tech giant with its chip division skyrocketing to record profit but its consumer electronics units languishing as chip costs surge.
Samsung's decision to shift SEA's headquarters is striking because SEA employees in New Jersey only moved to new offices with much fanfare less than a year ago. SEA employs about 1,200 workers in New Jersey, according to a press release by US Representative Josh Gottheimer, who attended an event to mark the opening of the new offices in September.
While the precise extent of the layoffs at SEA could not be learned, documents seen by Reuters show that the unit notified some employees on June 30 of an "enterprise-wide reduction-in-force", adding that there were a "significant number of impacts."
LinkedIn posts reviewed by Reuters also show more than 30 workers, including senior sales and marketing officials in both Texas and New Jersey as well as a few in other US locations, said they have been let go or left the company over the past couple of weeks.
Details about the job losses at SEA have not previously been reported.
Samsung said in its statement that the shift of SEA's headquarters "may lead to changes in our workforce structure, such as employees who are unable to relocate, or certain functions that are optimized to ensure our roles align to key business priorities."
CHIP DIVISION SOARS, BUT MOBILE MAY POST LOSS
Samsung has flagged it will likely post a 19-fold jump in second-quarter profit on strong AI-driven chip demand. It also announced plans last month to invest hundreds of billions of dollars in new chip plants.
Its mobile division, however, is expected to post its first-ever loss, as it grapples with intense competition from Apple. Chinese rivals like TCL and Hisense are also challenging Samsung in televisions and home appliances. Higher chip costs due to the AI boom have weighed on earnings for all of its consumer electronics products.
Samsung's job cuts mirror moves by other global firms including Microsoft, Amazon and Meta, which have shed jobs while redirecting spending toward AI infrastructure.
It has also joined Tesla, Oracle and other tech companies in moving headquarters or major operations to Texas, known for lower taxes and business-friendly regulations. The state is already home to Samsung's chip factories and a mobile hub in Plano.
Samsung workers are concerned the recent job cuts could be followed by additional layoffs and a consolidation of the company's appliance, home entertainment and mobile divisions, as it focuses resources on chips, a current SEA employee said.
Samsung said in its statement there was currently no broad, global restructuring underway within its consumer product business.
The relocation of the unit's headquarters is intended to foster "stronger collaboration and optimize the organization by bringing more teams together within a growing technology and AI ecosystem," it said.
Samsung Electronics had 11,770 employees in the United States as of the end of 2025, which includes workers in its chip division.
Samsung's IT services affiliate Samsung SDS America , has flagged that 179 roles could be cut at Ridgefield Park, New Jersey, according to a June notice required under the state's laws.
Those personnel changes are due to the relocation of Samsung SDS' North American headquarters and had nothing to do with layoffs or restructuring, Samsung said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Syed Saddiq to focus on marriage before deciding on Muda leadership return
Article:
MUAR: Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has yet to consider reclaiming the leadership of Muda following his recent court acquittal.
Instead, the former youth and sports minister said he would focus on the next chapter of his life, which is building a family.
He said the decision was made after spending more than a decade focusing entirely on politics since 2014.
Syed Saddiq is engaged to actress and singer
Bella Astillah
.
"Honestly, right now I want to focus on building a family first. This is not a safe answer; it is an honest and sincere answer, straight from the heart," he said after presenting aid at the Muar parliamentary service centre here today.
"Since I started in politics in 2014 or 2015, I have not stopped. During that period, it was not just me going through it; my whole family went through the same thing," he said.
In November 2023, Syed Saddiq announced he was
stepping down as Muda president
with immediate effect to clear his name following a court conviction, adding that he would not be involved in party politics while his court case was ongoing.
However, he assured that he would continue to support Muda as an ordinary member and work closely with its members.
Meanwhile, he clarified that he would never neglect his duties as an MP to fulfil the mandate given by the people. He said his responsibilities in Parliament and as an ordinary Muda member would continue until Parliament is dissolved.
"Muda today is led by Amira Aisya (Abd Aziz). The party doesn't belong to me alone because there is an election process that needs to be followed," he said.
Syed Saddiq is the founder and former president of Muda, a youth-focused political party he established in 2020.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pesona Metro bags RM247.5mil hospital job
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pesona Metro Holdings Bhd has secured a RM247.5 million contract to build a private hospital in Setia Alam, Selangor.
Pesona Metro said its wholly-owned Pesona Metro Sdn Bhd had accepted a Letter of Award from Setia Alam Medical Centre Sdn Bhd (SAMC) for the execution and completion of piling, basement, main building and external works for the project.
"The project comprises a basement level accommodating car park bays together with mechanical and electrical space and supporting facilities, along with three blocks of varying heights," it said.
SAMC is a subsidiary of Mega First Corp Bhd and is principally involved in the development and operation of a medical centre.
Pesona Metro managing director Wie Hock Beng said the contract marked another milestone for the company as it continued to expand its footprint in healthcare-related construction.
Wie said with the latest win, Pesona Metro's outstanding order book increased to RM2.4 billion across nine ongoing projects, providing the company with continued earnings visibility over the medium term.
"Moving forward, we remain focused on replenishing and growing our construction order book, balancing risk and reward in assessing new opportunities," he said.
The proposed development comprises three hospital blocks with supporting facilities and multi-storey car parks, located in Setia Alam, Shah Alam, Selangor.
Construction is scheduled to commence on July 28 this year and is expected to be completed within 30 months by Jan 28, 2029.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Resign if you want to attack unity partners, Anwar tells ministers
Article:
IPOH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned ministers, deputy ministers and heads of federal agencies to resign if they intend to use their federal positions to attack unity government partners during state election campaigns.
Anwar said while political parties aligned at the federal level were free to contest against one another in state elections, those holding federal office must maintain discipline and avoid turning their positions into political platforms.
"If they wish to do so, they should first resign from their positions as ministers, deputy ministers or members of agency boards," he said.
He said this to reporters after attending the Madani Kita Programme at Dewan Komuniti Taman Perpaduan here today.
Anwar added that the reminder was conveyed during his discussions yesterday with Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, following the ongoing election campaign in Negri Sembilan.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KWAP details response to eFishery fallout, RM163.4mil hit by fraud
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kumpulan Wang Persaraan (Diperbadankan) (KWAP) has sought to reassure stakeholders following recent media reports on its investment in Indonesian aquaculture startup eFishery.
KWAP said it had strengthened its private market investment framework while pursuing avenues to recover its investment.
RM163.4mil investment hit by fraud
The pension fund said the wider eFishery scandal involved deliberate financial manipulation and misrepresentation of the company's financial reporting.
On April 29, eFishery co-founder and former chief executive officer Gibran Huzaifah was sentenced by the Bandung District Court to nine years' imprisonment after being convicted of embezzlement and money laundering.
"In light of the matters raised, we wish to provide further clarity on the actions undertaken following our internal review, as well as the measures being implemented to safeguard the interests of the Fund," KWAP said.
KWAP said its total investment in eFishery amounted to RM163.4 million, representing about 2.51 per cent of the company's total shareholding.
It noted that it was a minority shareholder, while the majority of shares were held by other investors, including major global institutional investors that were similarly affected by the misconduct.
Recovery Efforts Underway
Following the discovery of the irregularities, KWAP said it conducted an internal investigation and reviewed its investment processes, post-investment monitoring arrangements and the information available throughout the investment period.
"Appropriate follow-up actions have been taken in accordance with KWAP's internal governance and accountability framework. KWAP is also continuing to pursue all available avenues to maximise the recovery of its investment," it said.
KWAP said it has since strengthened its private market investment approach.
This is through greater portfolio diversification, investments alongside experienced fund managers and strategic partners, enhanced post-investment monitoring, and closer oversight of material developments involving portfolio companies.
The fund stressed that its portfolio remains diversified across asset classes, sectors and geographies.
Based on its unaudited results for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025, KWAP recorded gross investment income of RM8.33 billion, with total funds under management of RM195.26 billion.
"KWAP remains committed to managing the Fund prudently, transparently, and responsibly in fulfilment of its statutory mandate to assist the government in meeting its pension obligations to public sector retirees," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: N9 polls: Act now or lose chance forever, says Tok Mat
Article:
SEREMBAN: The understanding between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) in Negri Sembilan is an important effort to safeguard the future of race and religion, Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said.
He said the initiative was first mooted in 2018 but did not materialise.
"I reached an understanding that, to me, is very important for our race and religion. In Negri Sembilan, I started it in 2018, but somehow it did not materialise and fell through halfway.
"I said that if we don't do it this time, we will miss the boat. We will be disappointed because our children and grandchildren will become mere traders in their own country. I don't want that to happen," he said after attending the Sendayan Air Base Family Day Carnival today.
Mohamad, who is also the Negri Sembilan BN chairman, said the effort must begin now, warning that failing to do so would mean losing the opportunity forever.
"If we don't start today, we will miss that opportunity forever. So in Negri Sembilan, I am fostering understanding to bring people together. This is for the future of our children and grandchildren, and let us all ensure this effort succeeds," he said.
At the same event, Mohamad also recounted how the development of Sendayan, which had stalled due to an economic downturn, was eventually completed after he resolved compensation issues involving residents.
Mohamad, who is also the BN candidate for the Rantau state seat, said the project has transformed Sendayan into one of the state's fastest-growing residential areas, attracting buyers from outside Negri Sembilan.
"Today, Sendayan has become what many describe as the Damansara of Seremban. Alhamdulillah, it is a development that I personally planned," he said.
He said that every aspect of the township's development was carefully planned, including housing designs and public amenities, to ensure the comfort and well-being of residents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas pitches broad coalition ahead of 16th General Election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas has proposed the formation of a broad political coalition involving Barisan Nasional, Perikatan Nasional and parties from Sabah and Sarawak, saying such an alliance would provide greater political stability and strengthen Malay-Muslim unity ahead of the 16th General Election.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said the party hoped recent electoral cooperation between BN and PN in Johor and Negri Sembilan would pave the way for a wider political understanding involving Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS).
"Of course, if we can establish a 'big tent' coalition involving BN, PN and parties from Sabah and Sarawak such as GRS and GPS, I believe that would be the best outcome.
"It would give the people a clear picture of the country's political direction," he said in an interview with Berita Harian.
Tuan Ibrahim said Pas believed the Johor election had demonstrated the benefits of electoral cooperation between BN and PN, despite the party failing to win any of the seats it contested.
He said Pas entered the election primarily to prevent Pakatan Harapan, particularly DAP, from expanding its influence in Johor.
"When Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang instructed our supporters to back BN candidates in seats not contested by PN, the strategy clearly produced results.
"We hope this becomes the starting point for uniting the two major Malay parties, Pas and Umno."
He said the division of the Malay vote had reached a critical stage and needed to be addressed before the next general election.
"It is time for the two main Malay parties to sit down together and find a political approach that does not disadvantage the Malay community.
"We have already seen this approach adopted in the Negri Sembilan election, and we hope it can be strengthened further ahead of GE16."
Tuan Ibrahim acknowledged that previous efforts under Muafakat Nasional had failed largely due to strained relations between Umno and Bersatu.
"We have to admit that relations between Umno and Bersatu remain difficult, making consensus challenging.
"Pas has tried to act as a mediator, although there are weaknesses and significant obstacles. Even so, we should not stop trying."
He described Muafakat Nasional as a political brand that had been widely accepted by Malays despite its relatively short lifespan.
"We are now working towards Muafakat Nasional 2.0, although participation may differ from before."
Asked whether Pas would accept Umno as the dominant Malay political party if cooperation resumed, Tuan Ibrahim said leadership should not be the primary issue.
"What matters is political understanding, not which party leads.
"There are states where Umno is strong and others where it is weak. Continued division will only create opportunities for our political opponents."
He also dismissed suggestions that Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative should be viewed as cooperation with PH.
"The name is not important. What matters is agreeing on the principles and foundations of cooperation.
"We can differ on other matters as long as we share the same core objectives."
Tuan Ibrahim said Johor and Negri Sembilan would serve as important tests of whether electoral cooperation between BN and PN could reduce vote splitting and improve opposition prospects against PH.
He added that any future political cooperation must be founded on integrity rather than power-sharing alone.
"Political cooperation must never become a pathway for corruption, abuse of power or misconduct.
"Politics is not merely about winning elections. It is about guiding the country towards what is right and beneficial."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Akmal Saleh defends BN-PN understanding, hits back at PH criticism
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has questioned PKR Youth chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim's call for Pakatan Harapan to review its cooperation with Barisan Nasional in the federal government following BN's electoral understanding with Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan election.
Dr Akmal, who is also Merlimau assemblyman, said Kamil should not single out BN over the arrangement, arguing that similar political cooperation had taken place in previous elections without triggering similar concerns.
He cited the Sabah state election, where Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) contested against PH despite being linked to the federal government, as an example of political competition between allies at different levels.
"I want to ask him, during the Sabah election, GRS contested against PH.
"Although Warisan was part of the federal government, and there was even a deputy minister from GRS, they still contested against the PH coalition.
"But at that time, there were no calls or suggestions to review the relationship," he told Berita Harian.
On Saturday, Kamil urged PH to reassess its political cooperation with BN in the unity government following the cooperation between the coalition led by Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and PN in Negri Sembilan.
A similar call was made by Amanah Youth chief Mohd Hasbie Muda, who said BN would have no reason to remain in the unity government if political cooperation with PN was indeed taking place.
Dr Akmal questioned whether Kamil would make the same call ahead of the Sarawak election.
"The Sarawak election is not far away. GPS (Gabungan Parti Sarawak) will certainly be contesting against PH there.
"So, should the relationship between PH and GPS at the federal level also be reviewed?" he asked.
Dr Akmal said he had consistently maintained that Umno and BN should leave the unity government, as the administration led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim could continue without the coalition.
"As I said in my speech at the Umno Youth Special Convention, we do not want to bring down the government through a backdoor. If BN leaves, the government can continue to function as usual," he said.
In the Negri Sembilan election, BN is contesting 25 of the 36 state seats.
The remaining 11 seats are being contested by PN, including five by Pas and four by Wawasan.
Earlier, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Umno president, described the cooperation with PN in the 16th Negri Sembilan state election as an understanding rather than an agreement or formal pact.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Syed Saddiq pledges 100pct of RM650,959 raised from gratitude run to Muar residents
Article:
MUAR: Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman concluded his four-day gratitude run today, raising RM650,959.21, more than three times his initial target.
The Muar member of parliament said the entire amount would be distributed to eligible recipients in the constituency and would not be used for administrative purposes.
He said the money entrusted by the public would not be touched, even to fund the operations or staff expenses of the Muar parliamentary service centre.
"That is why I said we will give full commitment and 100 per cent transparency. In fact, we will prepare a report presenting the receipts spent for the first phase tonight," he said when met after presenting aid at the parliamentary service centre.
Syed Saddiq said he had instructed his team not to take a single sen from the collection, although normal non-governmental organisation practices allowed up to 30 per cent of collections to be used for administrative expenses.
"We must return 100 per cent to them. I will pay the staff salaries using my own money or find other ways. We will definitely not use this money," he said.
Also present was his fiancée, actress and singer Bella Astillah.
Syed Saddiq said he would also announce several other aid packages, including the National Education Savings Scheme (Simpan SSPN) for youths to help them pursue higher education.
Earlier, he posted on Facebook that he would distribute 1,000 food baskets, 51 iPads, and RM61,400 in education scholarships at noon today.
Syed Saddiq, who
completed the 170km run this morning
, said he was indebted to the people of Muar for their support.
He said when he was just 10km away from the finish line, he received support from people from all walks of life despite the hot weather.
"They were willing to wait, and when I arrived at Laman Maharani, I was surprised to see a sea of people on both sides, to the point where I could hardly move," he said.
"At times I felt exhausted, my muscles were cramping, and I felt like fainting, but the support given somehow gave me the energy to continue the four-day run.
"Initially, we targeted RM200,000, but we managed to collect more than three times our target. They are ordinary citizens who did not even have to donate," he said.
He added that most of the donors were not from Muar, but as Malaysians, they wanted to help the people of Muar who needed assistance.
Syed Saddiq began the four-day run from the Palace of Justice compound in Putrajaya to Muar as a gesture of appreciation to supporters who stood by him
throughout his court case
, marking the end of his legal battle to clear his name.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azalina: Political cooperation must put people first
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has played down speculation over closer ties with Pas following Barisan Nasional's landslide victory in Johor, saying any future political cooperation must be guided by sincerity and the interests of the people rather than the pursuit of power.
Umno information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said BN remained committed to the Unity Government and dismissed suggestions that the coalition was seeking political cooperation merely to consolidate power.
She said any future understanding with other parties should be driven by shared goals for the rakyat rather than political expediency.
"Any form of cooperation must be assessed based on sincerity and a common objective of serving the people, not simply to gather numbers for political power.
"BN is not a coalition that engages in covert political manoeuvres or attempts to split parties from within because that is what ultimately destroys cooperation," she said in an interview with Berita Harian.
Her remarks come amid growing speculation over closer ties between BN and Perikatan Nasional, particularly between Umno and Pas, following the Johor election and recent electoral arrangements involving BN and PN in Negri Sembilan.
However, Azalina stressed that decisions on political cooperation were not for individuals to make but rested with Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and the party's Supreme Council.
"This is not a question that can be answered by any individual. The direction of relations with other parties is a collective decision of the party leadership.
"What I can say is that Umno and BN's priority now is to strengthen ourselves. A strong party is open to discussions because of its strength, not because it is desperate."
She also reaffirmed BN's commitment to the Unity Government, saying the coalition remained in the federal administration in line with the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's decree to ensure political stability and effective governance.
On BN's commanding victory in Johor, Azalina said the outcome reflected growing public confidence in the coalition's performance rather than racial or religious rhetoric.
BN won 48 of the 56 state seats contested, increasing its share of the popular vote from 43 per cent in the 2022 state election to almost 60 per cent.
"This is not merely about numbers. It is a signal that voters, including Malay voters, are returning to politics based on performance and service.
"The BN government in Johor delivered during its term, and that is why the people voted for it."
Azalina also rejected suggestions that Umno's political objective was to contain Pakatan Harapan (PH) or DAP.
"Umno has never built its politics around becoming a barrier against any political party.
"In every election, political parties present their record and manifesto. It is for the people to decide."
She said BN's strength, as demonstrated in Johor, stemmed from its ability to win support across racial lines rather than relying on communal narratives.
Commenting on Umno's Rumah Bangsa initiative and the Musyawarah Nasional platform, Azalina said neither should be interpreted as an effort to pave the way for formal political cooperation with Pas.
She said Rumah Bangsa was intended to welcome former Umno members and others interested in joining the party, while Musyawarah Nasional served as an independent platform bringing together academics, professionals, non-governmental organisations and political parties to discuss issues affecting the Malay community.
"There is no political agenda behind Musyawarah Nasional."
Looking ahead to the 16th General Election, Azalina cautioned against assuming Johor's outcome would automatically translate into success at the national level.
"State election results cannot automatically be translated into the national political landscape.
"Candidates, national issues, voter turnout and voting patterns can all change.
"What BN can control is its own efforts — continuing to serve the people, fulfilling its promises and proving its record through results.
"If BN remains consistent, I believe the momentum will sustain itself because people judge performance, not promises."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Survivors of Indonesia boat sinking rescued after days at sea
Article:
JAKARTA: Five survivors from a passenger boat that sank off eastern Indonesia, including a seven-year-old girl, have been rescued after floating at sea for three days, a rescue official said on Sunday.
The vessel, which was carrying more than 70 people, sank on Wednesday as it sailed near Selayar, a small island south of the larger Sulawesi.
Survivors told rescuers they had been part of a group of 25 people earlier reported as missing before becoming separated from the others by heavy winds.
"After the ship sank, each of them saved themselves using whatever equipment or makeshift flotation they could find," local search-and-rescue official Muhammad Arif Anwar told AFP.
"They rigged together jerry cans and pieces of cork tied up with rope, then climbed on top of them."
The group was rescued on Saturday afternoon by local fishermen, who radioed officials for help, he said.
The survivors appeared tired and weak after floating for days without enough food or water, Arif said, but had been able to sustain themselves with packets of instant noodles and biscuits.
Five large ships, a reconnaissance aircraft and a helicopter are being used in ongoing search efforts for those still missing, he said.
Rescuers said the vessel listed only 50 people on board, but further verification found there were 78 on the ship.
It is common in Indonesia for the number of actual passengers on a boat to differ from the manifest. Accidents are a regular occurrence, due in part to lax safety standards and inclement weather.
Three Spanish tourists died when their boat sank off eastern Indonesia in January. A 10-year-old boy was listed as officially missing after authorities ended the search.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia must prepare now for expected Super El Niño in November
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must begin preparing now for the possibility of a Super El Niño expected to develop from November, with early action crucial to minimising its impact on lives, livelihoods and the environment.
Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said forecasts indicating the country could experience a Super El Niño, with conditions expected to peak between November and January, should be treated as a serious warning and a call to action rather than a cause for panic.
"Previous El Niño episodes had demonstrated that prolonged hot and dry weather could trigger water shortages, forest and peatland fires, transboundary haze, reduced agricultural yields, food security concerns, health risks and disruptions to economic activities.
"The expected Super El Niño should therefore be viewed not merely as a seasonal phenomenon but as another reminder that Malaysia must strengthen its climate resilience and disaster preparedness," he said in a statement.
Lee said vulnerable groups, including senior citizens, young children, outdoor workers and people with chronic illnesses, would face greater risks from heat stress and dehydration during prolonged periods of extreme heat.
"The government should adopt a proactive, coordinated and whole-of-society approach to mitigate the effects of the weather phenomenon," he said.
Among the measures proposed were strengthening water resource management through prudent water use, reducing leakages, expanding storage capacity and preparing contingency plans for areas vulnerable to shortages.
He also called for stricter monitoring and enforcement to prevent open burning and forest or peatland fires, while ensuring rapid emergency response capabilities.
"Public health preparedness should be enhanced through timely heat advisories, the establishment of cooling centres where necessary and public education on preventing heat-related illnesses.
"Support for farmers through efficient irrigation systems, drought-resistant crops, technical assistance and financial aid to minimise losses is also needed," he said.
Lee also called for the importance of ensuring uninterrupted electricity supply to meet higher demand for cooling during prolonged hot weather, expanding public awareness campaigns on water conservation and fire prevention, and strengthening coordination among federal, state and local authorities to enable swift emergency responses.
"Preparedness is always more effective and less costly than responding to a crisis after it has occurred.
"With early planning and strong cooperation among government agencies, businesses, communities and the public, Malaysia can reduce the impact of the expected Super El Niño and better protect lives, livelihoods and the environment," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah royals meet Help University blast survivor Reeya
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Help University blast survivor Reeya Kaur Thandal and her family received a royal gesture of support when the Kedah royal family met them at Istana Kedah here, offering encouragement as the 19-year-old continues her recovery from serious injuries.
The meeting on Saturday was attended by the Raja Muda of Kedah Tengku Sarafudin Badlishah Ibni Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin, the Raja Puan Muda of Kedah Che Puan Muda Zaheeda Mohamad Ariff, and their children, Tunku Zara Bahiyah and Tunku Sulaiman Badlishah.
The gathering was held as a gesture of concern and moral support for Reeya and her family, who have been going through a challenging period following the incident.
The royal family spent time speaking with Reeya and her parents, enquiring about her condition and offering words of encouragement as she continues her recovery.
Reeya, whose mother is a Kedah native, received widespread public attention after she suffered serious injuries in an
explosion at Help University
in Bukit Damansara on Jan 12.
The incident occurred when maintenance work was being carried out at the university's air-conditioning compressor room, resulting in the death of a contractor and injuries to nine others.
The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department said initial investigations found that the blast originated from the compressor room located within the cafeteria area.
The explosion caused about 30 per cent damage to the fourth floor of the building but did not result in a fire.
Reeya, then a first-year law student, suffered serious injuries, including skull fractures and brain bleeding, following the incident.
Earlier on Jan 16, Che Puan Muda Zaheeda
visited Reeya
at the Tunku Abdul Rahman Neurology Ward in Hospital Kuala Lumpur.
Che Puan Muda Zaheeda, who is also the royal patron of the Malaysian Mental Health Association, is actively involved in initiatives promoting mental health awareness and support.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hadi calls for Malay-Muslim unity with 'non-extreme' partners including MCA, MIC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has urged voters to support a political formula built around cooperation between Pas, Umno, and "non-extreme" non-Muslim parties, including MCA and MIC, as he called for a wider unity movement to "save Malaysia."
In a Facebook post today, Abdul Hadi said the approach was based on the principles of unity and cooperation between Malay Muslims, Bumiputera communities and non-Muslims who, he said, did not adopt extreme positions.
"Pas calls for change that follows the example of Prophet Muhammad and is based on the strength of unity between Muslims and non-Muslims who are not extreme.
"This should begin with Pas together with Umno, Barisan Nasional, Malay-Muslim groups and non-Muslim parties that are not extreme, such as MCA, MIC, the Dewan Himpunan Pendukung Pas (DHPP) and others," he said.
He added that the cooperation should eventually extend to Sabah and Sarawak as part of efforts to "save Malaysia."
"This should then be followed by Sarawak and Sabah to save our beloved Malaysia."
Abdul Hadi said that, in the Malaysian context, Malay Muslims should play a leading role as they form the majority of the country's Muslim population.
He urged voters to reject what he described as "unnatural Malay groups" that cooperate with DAP, claiming such groups represented secular and extreme elements that could threaten Malay and Islamic interests.
"In Malaysia, Malay Muslims must lead because they dominate the Muslim population.
"The existence of different races is part of Allah's creation to test humanity. Reject unnatural Malay groups that are with DAP, namely fanatical secular and extreme chauvinist groups that threaten Islam and the Malay community," he said.
However, he said this position was not based on racial fanaticism, but on what he described as Islam's obligation to uphold justice for all communities.
His remarks come amid closer cooperation between Pas and Umno in the Negri Sembilan election, where Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional have avoided direct clashes in the distribution of seats.
The arrangement has raised questions over whether the cooperation could evolve into a wider political realignment ahead of the 16th General Election.
In Negri Sembilan, BN is contesting 25 of the 36 state seats, while PN is fielding candidates in 11 constituencies. Pas is contesting five seats under the PN banner.
The cooperation has also sparked debate within Pakatan Harapan, with some leaders questioning whether BN's continued participation in the Federal Government's unity arrangement is compatible with its cooperation with PN at the state level.
Umno leaders have maintained that the arrangement in Negri Sembilan is a strategic understanding aimed at avoiding multi-cornered contests and preventing vote splitting, rather than a formal political merger.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SKDS registrations reach 172,955 companies nationwide
Article:
LABUAN: A total of 172,955 companies involving 464,797 vehicles have registered under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) nationwide as of July 18.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said this included 28,127 companies involving 68,669 vehicles registered in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan following the expansion of the targeted diesel subsidy mechanism to the three regions on July 1.
Of the total, 18,770 companies with 51,679 vehicles were registered under the land transport sector for consumer goods, while 7,241 companies involving 13,557 vehicles were registered under the company-owned land transport sector covering jeeps and pickup trucks.
Another 2,116 companies involving 3,433 vehicles were registered under the public land transport sector.
"The continued increase in registrations reflects encouraging progress in the implementation of SKDS in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan," Armizan said at a press conference after the SKDS fleet card presentation ceremony at Kampung Patau-Patau 2 here today.
"For companies that have been successfully registered and approved under SKDS, the next step is to apply for a fleet card from the participating oil companies," he said.
Earlier, representatives of three participating fuel companies, namely Petronas, Shell and Petron, accompanied Armizan in presenting the fleet cards to eligible companies in Labuan.
Armizan said the use of fleet cards allowed every subsidised diesel purchase transaction to be digitally recorded, thereby strengthening monitoring, reducing the risk of leakages and ensuring the subsidy benefited the intended groups.
He urged eligible companies that had yet to register to submit their applications through the MySubsidi portal.
Applications for SKDS in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan opened on May 4, ahead of the implementation of the targeted diesel subsidy mechanism.
Armizan said the ministry was holding discussions with the Finance Ministry to examine proposals to improve SKDS based on implementation experience, industry feedback and operational issues.
He said engagement sessions with the Sabah and Sarawak governments would continue to ensure operational requirements on the ground were considered without compromising the objectives of subsidy targeting and efforts to curb leakages.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wife of Indian activist Wangchuk asks court to move him to private hospital
Article:
NEW DELHI: Social activist Sonam Wangchuk's wife has approached an Indian court seeking permission to transfer him from a government hospital to a private facility, alleging he was being held in "illegal detention," she said on Sunday.
"Despite repeated requests, the hospital has refused to discharge him or allow us to shift him to a private hospital of our choice," Gitanjali Angmo said in a post on social media website X, a day after Wangchuk was moved to state-run Safdarjung Hospital against his wishes by security personnel.
The hospital said on Sunday that Wangchuk's vital parameters were stable, although his blood parameters remained "marginally altered", and that he required sustained medical intervention and round-the-clock monitoring.
Phone calls to the hospital and its director's office went unanswered.
Wangchuk, 59, had been fasting since June 28 in solidarity with India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), which was formed by Indian youths and is demanding federal Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan step down over exam paper leaks in May that affected millions of students.
The campaign has emerged as a rare public challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government and has drawn support across India.
Wangchuk had told government doctors on Friday that he did not wish to be moved to a hospital. On Saturday, he was refusing ​treatment, hospital authorities had said.
"With around 30 police personnel stationed on our floor and well over 100 across the hospital, our movement is severely restricted," Angmo said on Sunday.
The Delhi Police, which reports to India's federal home ministry, did not immediately respond to a phone call and email seeking comment.
On Thursday, the Delhi High Court asked authorities to watch Wangchuk's health closely and intervene if needed, in response to a petition asking authorities to force-feed him as his health weakened.
The CJP plans to march to Parliament on Monday, the first day of its latest session, to press its demands.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China's Jingye warns of legal action after nationalisation of British Steel
Article:
BEIJING: Jingye Steel demanded compensation and threatened the UK government with legal action on Sunday, after London took control of British Steel from the Chinese firm.
Jingye, which bought British Steel in 2020, lost control of it last year when the UK government intervened at the struggling firm.
Britain's last factory capable of making steel from scratch had faced imminent closure after Jingye said the plant in Scunthorpe, northern England, was no longer financially viable.
The announcement prompted the government to step in and take control of it in April 2025, and on Thursday, the government announced that it had brought it into full public ownership.
But Jingye threatened legal action against the move and accused the UK government of "trampling on international investment rules" and "outright robbery" as it demanded compensation for "investment losses" in a statement on its official WeChat account on Sunday.
"Jingye reserves all legal rights, firmly defends its lawful rights and interests, and will pursue full compensation through legal means to the very end," the company said.
The UK government said Thursday it had taken British Steel into full public ownership to "protect the future of steel production in the UK", after taking control from Jingye.
On Friday, China's commerce ministry said it opposed the move, and accused the UK of "forcibly" taking over the company "under the pretext of national security."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Activist remanded two days over criminal threat on Facebook
Article:
SEREMBAN: An activist suspected of making a criminal threat against a man on Facebook has been remanded for two days to assist in investigations.
The remand order against the 34-year-old man was issued by magistrate Naeilah Nikrul Tarmizi at the magistrate's court here today.
The suspect is being investigated under Section 506 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.
Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad yesterday said the man surrendered at the Port Dickson district police headquarters after fleeing to Thailand in March.
He said police received a report from a man at 6.25pm on March 10, claiming he had been threatened by the suspect on the social media platform.
Subsequent efforts to trace the suspect in Kajang, Selangor, were unsuccessful.
The suspect was believed to have fled to Thailand via Bukit Kayu Hitam on March 9. He recently re-entered Malaysia through an illegal route at the Kelantan border before surrendering.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cousins put politics aside during campaign trail encounter
Article:
JELEBU: "Kirim salam kek ayah" (send my regards to your father).
That was the spontaneous message from Pakatan Harapan candidate for the Klawang state seat, Datuk Bakri Sawir, to his Perikatan Nasional counterpart Danni Rais when the two cousins crossed paths during campaigning at the Kuala Klawang Sunday Market today.
The two exchanged greetings and chatted briefly before Bakri asked Danni to convey his regards to his uncle, Tan Sri Dr Rais Yatim.
Bakri said he had no issue greeting his cousin whenever they met during the campaign period, despite representing opposing political camps.
"Our family ties remain strong even though we have different political ideologies," he said.
At 67, Bakri maintains an active lifestyle through regular exercise and healthy habits, including walking about 700 metres from his home to Masjid Jamek Kuala Klawang for daily Subuh prayers.
He is also active in hiking and pays attention to his diet.
"I hope to complete the demanding two-week campaign schedule well and remain healthy, InsyaAllah," he said.
On development plans for Kuala Klawang, Bakri said several initiatives would continue if he is re-elected as the assemblyman for a third term.
Among them is the proposed modern rest and recreation (R&R) stop in Kampung Tembun, which was put on hold due to land status issues.
"There are several matters that need to be resolved before the fully equipped R&R can be built, allowing Kuala Klawang to have a proper stop for road users.
"The four-hectare land was found to be Malay reserve land, so the plan had to be postponed for the time being," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Russian missiles strike Kyiv killing one, wounding 13
Article:
KYIV: Russian missiles struck the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and the surrounding region early on Sunday, killing at least one person and wounding 13 others as fires broke out across the city, officials said.
A series of powerful explosions thundered in the night, as Ukraine's air force warned of a ballistic missile threat.
Fires broke out at a dormitory, an apartment building and a supermarket, Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said on the Telegram app.
Several non-residential buildings and warehouses were struck in the attack, while parked cars and office buildings were on fire in several districts, Klitschko said.
At one location, workers picked through smouldering debris and doused bombed-out apartments.
[[nid:1492300]]
A resident who identified himself as Vlad said he had been inside his apartment when a blast tore off his balcony door, which smashed him in the head.
"My grandmother lives with me, and she can't walk. How could I run away and leave her behind?" he said.
The State Emergency Service dispatched emergency workers to several locations across three city districts.
Two people were wounded in the Kyiv region, according to the military administration, and warehouses there were also damaged.
Russian forces have stepped up ballistic missile strikes on Kyiv and other cities in recent weeks, as Ukraine runs low on critical US-designed air defences to shoot them down.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Water treatment waste turned into high-value bricks in Penang
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Water treatment plant residue, previously classified as scheduled waste, is being transformed into high-value construction materials through an innovative circular economy initiative.
The breakthrough follows a collaboration between Alam Aliran Kualiti (M) Sdn Bhd (AAK) and the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP), which has successfully converted the SW204 scheduled waste into raw material for cement brick production.
The initiative makes PBAPP the first water operator in Malaysia to adopt a circular economy model for managing water treatment plant residue, while AAK is the country's first facility to process SW204 into brick material.
Beyond reducing reliance on landfill disposal, the project demonstrates how waste with no prior commercial value can be converted into quality construction products through sustainable technology.
AAK project head G. Karthigayan said the technology was developed through a strategic partnership with PBAPP to provide a sustainable solution for residue management.
"Our objective is not only to help water operators manage residue more efficiently, but also to change the perception that water treatment waste is merely something to be discarded.
"With the right technology, strategy and management approach, this waste can be transformed into a resource with economic value," he said.
He explained that the process begins at the Sungai Dua Dewatering Plant (SDDP), where residue from the water treatment process undergoes belt press dewatering technology to separate water from solids.
The recovered water is returned to PBAPP's water supply system, while the remaining alum sludge is transported to AAK's brick manufacturing facility in Bukit Minyak.
At the factory, the sludge undergoes chemical treatment to produce aggregates, which are used as the primary raw material for manufacturing cement bricks using conventional production methods and a proprietary formulation developed by the company to meet industry standards.
"This technology enables the recovery of about nine million litres of water per day that would otherwise have been discharged into the river. The volume is sufficient to meet the daily water needs of between 10,000 and 12,000 households," he said.
In addition to reducing landfill waste, Karthigayan said tests showed that the bricks produced using recycled sludge offered better fire resistance than conventional cement bricks.
The plant has the capacity to produce up to 150,000 bricks daily, though current production ranges between 70,000 and 100,000 bricks, depending on the volume of sludge supplied by SDDP.
The bricks have been commercialised and are being used in construction projects while also being sold through hardware stores across Penang.
Karthigayan said the technology was developed over nearly five years of intensive research and development, supported by extensive technical evaluations and regulatory approvals, as SW204 is classified as scheduled waste under the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
He added that the product has obtained ISO 9001 certification and is undergoing certification by the Standards and Industrial Research Institute of Malaysia (Sirim) and the Fire and Rescue Department to expand its use within the construction industry.
The project is implemented under a 15-year design, build, operate and maintain contract.
AAK bears the full cost of capital investment, construction, operations and residue management, while PBAPP serves as the project owner and oversees auditing and compliance.
Karthigayan said the arrangement enabled PBAPP to reduce its operational and capital expenditure while ensuring AAK assumed full responsibility for scheduled waste management, environmental compliance and safety standards.
He added that AAK is set to be recognised by the Malaysia Book of Records in the Largest Belt Press System Dewatering Plant category for operating a 10-million-litre-per-day dewatering facility capable of processing up to 450 metric tonnes of sludge daily.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rosmah thanks supporters, seeks prayers for Najib's recovery [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has urged the public to pray for a smooth medical procedure for her husband, former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, who is scheduled to undergo treatment to remove plaque near a blood vessel.
Rosmah confirmed the matter in a Facebook post yesterday, expressing appreciation for the concern and support shown by Najib's friends and supporters.
"I greatly appreciate the concern shown by his friends and supporters. At this time, I ask everyone to continue praying that the procedure goes smoothly and that he is blessed with health and strength," she said.
She also thanked those who had sent prayers and words of encouragement to the family.
"Thank you for your continuous thoughts, love and prayers. May Allah protect him and grant him the best," she said.
It was reported on Friday that Najib would undergo a related procedure next week.
Najib, 73, is currently serving a prison sentence after being convicted in the SRC International case.
In February 2024, the Pardons Board reduced his prison term from 12 years to six years and lowered his fine from RM210 million to RM50 million.
In April, Najib withdrew his application for house arrest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: North Borneo stamps a living archive of Sabah history, says collector
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Efforts to preserve North Borneo stamps, now over a century old, are becoming increasingly crucial as these rare collectibles disappear, along with the historical records of Sabah's early development.
Borneo History Association founder and president Dr Shari Jeffri, 56, described the philatelic collections issued between 1883 and 1963 as a living archive that must be preserved for future generations.
"Today's generation is less exposed to stamp collecting than in the past. Now only a few are really interested, and the number of enthusiasts is also decreasing," he said.
A survey of several antique shops around Kota Kinabalu found that these valuable items are increasingly difficult to obtain and command high prices based on their age, condition, and rarity.
The survey uncovered an album containing a six-sen North Borneo stamp featuring Queen Elizabeth II alongside a Dusun woman, as well as a 10-sen stamp depicting logging activities issued between 1954 and 1961.
Sharing the passion behind his hobby, Shari said much of his collection was inherited from his late grandfather, who worked at the Jesselton Recreation Club in Kota Kinabalu during the 1920s.
His grandfather began collecting stamps and postal artefacts after observing British officers pursuing the hobby.
Shari was introduced to philately at the age of seven but became an active collector during his secondary school years. Since then, he has maintained his family's inherited collection, which fueled his research into the history and evolution of stamps in the North Borneo territory.
Among his most valuable possessions are two two-sen North Borneo stamps from the 1883 issue, featuring a brown sailing boat and bearing postmarks.
"For stamp collectors, a collection is considered incomplete without the 1883 issue. Every stamp is more than just a means of paying postage, but rather a story line recording the events of a particular era," he said.
Shari, who has been involved in philately for nearly 40 years, said North Borneo stamps were introduced in 1883 by the British North Borneo Chartered Company and remained in use for about 52 years.
The design changed over time, including updates in 1935 that more clearly reflected Sabah's identity. The stamps were issued in various denominations ranging from two sen to one dollar.
In the early stages, the stamps featured symbols such as lions, boats, and tigers until around 1892, alongside the Latin motto Pergo et Perago, which translates to 'I undertake and I achieve', to introduce the region to the world.
Beginning in 1894, the designs changed to feature Borneo's native flora, fauna, and wildlife. The biggest challenge today is ensuring they are stored in acid-free albums to prevent them from fading, he said.
Shari said stamps bearing complete postal cancellations are rare and more valuable because they display comprehensive information, including the mailing date, post office name, time, and location.
The use of stamp paper with a layer of glue is also an important feature in determining the authenticity and value of philatelic materials.
To deepen his knowledge, he sought guidance from Singapore experts Voon Kyam Foh and Tan Chun Lim and referred to specialised catalogues such as Commonwealth & British Empire Stamps.
Although the practice of exchanging letters has largely been displaced by digital communication, antique stamps from North Borneo remain invaluable witnesses to Sabah's historical journey.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor man loses RM1.1mil in scam involving fake prosecutor, bank officers
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A 63-year-old self-employed man lost RM1.1 million after falling victim to a phone scam involving fraudsters impersonating a prosecutor and Bank Negara Malaysia officers.
He lodged a police report yesterday after realising his entire savings had been wiped out.
The man's ordeal began on March 4 when he received a call from a woman claiming to be a prosecutor.
She told him he was allegedly linked to money laundering activities and was under criminal investigation.
He was then connected to several "Bank Negara officers", who instructed him to open a new bank account for investigation purposes and transfer his savings into it.
He was also instructed to bank in an undisclosed sum into the new account for court guarantees and document processing fees. He complied with the instructions.
Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said the victim was duped into making 51 transactions between March and May after being falsely accused of involvement in money laundering activities.
Azrul said the victim used his savings to make the transactions into several bank accounts provided by the suspects, amounting to RM1.1 million.
The victim only realised he had been deceived after checking with police and was informed that the modus operandi matched that of a phone scam syndicate impersonating enforcement agencies and financial institutions, he said in a statement.
Azrul reminded the public that no enforcement agency, prosecutor, court, Bank Negara Malaysia, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission or government ministry would instruct individuals to open new bank accounts, apply for loans, transfer money or disclose banking details through phone calls.
Members of the public were advised to verify claims directly with the relevant agencies and never share bank details, TAC, OTP or online banking information.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC, Health Ministry to launch drone medicine delivery pilot project in Tawau
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), in collaboration with the Health Ministry, will launch a drone medicine delivery pilot project in Tawau, Sabah, around September or October.
The initiative expands the role of the National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) in healthcare delivery.
MCMC Nadi Office head Dr Mohd Arif Adenan said the initiative, under the Nadi Smart Services programme, is expected to benefit residents in about 12 villages, particularly those in rural and remote areas who face difficulties obtaining regular medicine supplies.
The project will be implemented with the cooperation of the Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM), the Sabah government, and several other agencies through a whole-of-government approach to ensure smooth and safe operations.
"The programme will help reduce the burden of travelling to obtain medicines, while saving both time and transportation costs, especially for patients living far from hospitals," he said.
Arif said a Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) study found that residents in Sabah's interior often travel between 20km and 30km to reach hospitals, making transportation costs one of the biggest barriers to accessing healthcare services.
He added that MCMC would consider expanding the initiative to other areas with similar needs if the pilot project achieves its objectives.
Medicine delivery by post is already available through Pos Malaysia, with Nadi serving as a one-stop collection centre for medicines within local communities.
"We also provide basic health screening services at Nadi centres for patients requiring regular monitoring, reducing the need for routine hospital visits for checks such as blood pressure monitoring.
"If patients record abnormal health readings, Nadi will immediately connect them with the relevant hospital for further assessment, while the initiative also helps reduce travelling costs and ease congestion at healthcare facilities," he said.
Arif said the initiative reflects MCMC's efforts to transform Nadi from a digital connectivity hub into a community centre offering wider access to essential government and public services.
On Nadi's future development, Arif said MCMC has no plans to increase the number of physical centres as the one-state constituency, one-Nadi policy has ensured every state constituency nationwide has at least one centre.
Instead, the focus is on expanding Nadi 2.0 virtual services to reach communities located further away, following a Merdeka Centre study showing that most users live within 1km to 2km of existing centres.
He said MCMC is nevertheless studying the possibility of extending the Nadi network to locations without such facilities, including military camps and police barracks, where demand for digital services and community development programmes has been identified.
"International recognition from the International Telecommunication Union (ITU), including the World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS) Award in Geneva last year and designation as the 16th ITU Digital Transformation Centre this year, has created new opportunities for global collaboration.
"NADI also plans to introduce more micro-credential courses in collaboration with international institutions and organisations such as Cisco and Hewlett Packard (HP), enabling participants, particularly rural youths, to earn industry-recognised certifications that enhance their employability," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Construction work halted after methane gas leak detected
Article:
MIRI: Construction work at a site along Jalan Perdana, Tudan here was halted today after methane gas was detected leaking from a cylinder pile.
A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesperson said the Bintulu Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) team was deployed to the scene to carry out further checks.
"Initial checks by a Petros contractor revealed that the gas detected was methane, with a 100 per cent Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) reading.
"Following the discovery, the Bintulu Hazmat team was mobilised to identify if other gases were present before further action could be taken," the spokesperson said.
The department received a distress call at 7.45am, and a five-member team from the Lutong fire station was dispatched to the location with a Fire Rescue Tender vehicle.
Upon arrival at 7.57am, the operations team confirmed that gas was leaking from one of the cylinder piles at the construction site.
The spokesperson said firefighters in full personal protective equipment (PPE) conducted an initial assessment before establishing a safety perimeter around the pile to eliminate risks to workers and the public.
The team monitored the leak while the affected cylinder pile was temporarily sealed. The Petros contractor was also present to conduct detailed checks, and all construction activities were suspended during the monitoring process.
No accidents or injuries were reported, and the situation at the scene remained under control.
The operation involved five firefighters from the Lutong fire station, four police personnel, and four Petros representatives.
The situation was brought under control at 9.55am, and the operation ended completely at 10.04am after the site was declared safe.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: Be cautious when using AI to generate content featuring Jalur Gemilang
Article:
IPOH: Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil has reminded Malaysians to ensure the Jalur Gemilang is displayed correctly, especially when using artificial intelligence (AI) to generate content involving the national flag.
While the use of AI brought many benefits, Fahmi said the public must remain cautious to avoid inaccuracies when producing images or materials featuring the national flag.
He said every detail of the Jalur Gemilang, including its 14 stripes, must be represented accurately as it carried significant meaning.
"We also encourage Malaysians taking part in the flying Jalur Gemilang campaign to spend some time learning about the correct procedures and etiquette in flying the Jalur Gemilang.
"At the same time, everyone must be careful when generating content using AI so that there are no mistakes or shortcomings. The 14 stripes on the Jalur Gemilang are very important," he said.
Fahmi said this to reporters after attending the National Month and Fly Jalur Gemilang 2026 programme at the Sultan Azlan Shah Health Ministry Training Institute here today.
The event was launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the ministry would also work with the Malaysian Press Institute (MPI) and the Malaysian Media Council to engage media organisations in ensuring representations of the Jalur Gemilang, particularly those created using AI, were accurate throughout the National Month celebrations.
He encouraged the public to start flying the Jalur Gemilang from now until at least Sept 16 in conjunction with the National Day and Malaysia Day celebrations.
Various programmes would be organised, including the Fly the Jalur Gemilang campaign and the Jelajah Jago dan Merdeka initiative.
"These programmes will later be compiled on the Merdeka 360 website. We encourage our media friends to help promote the platform," he said.
Fahmi hoped the National Month celebrations would serve as more than just a ceremonial opening, but would ignite patriotism among Malaysians.
"I hope this event will not only serve as a curtain-raiser, but truly ignite the spirit of patriotism and raise the national spirit.
"It should help us not only remember the history of our independence and the formation of Malaysia, but also give meaning, especially to the younger generation, about the role we must play as citizens in safeguarding sovereignty, preserving unity and securing the future of our country," he said.
On action against those who incorrectly display or produce inaccurate representations of the Jalur Gemilang, Fahmi said the government's initial approach would be to provide advice and guidance.
He said there were existing laws related to the matter, but authorities would first engage with individuals or organisations involved to rectify the mistakes.
"Our first step is to advise. We will try to contact any party that has mistakenly produced content or incorrectly displayed the Jalur Gemilang and advise them to make corrections.
"If it is not corrected for any reason, we will then review what appropriate follow-up action should be taken. But we will begin with advice," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Agrobank expands Agro Penjaja-i's reach
Article:
Azanis Shahila Aman
KUALA LUMPUR:  Bank Pertanian Malaysia  Bhd (Agrobank) has formed a partnership with the Federation of Malay Hawkers and Small Traders Associations Malaysia to expand its Agro Penjaja-i initiative.
Agrobank president and group chief executive officer Tengku Datuk Ahmad Badli Shah Raja Hussin said small-time traders operate viable businesses but continue to face challenges in accessing capital, managing finances and adopting digital solutions.
Badli said the collaboration marks a key step for both parties to strengthen support for small-time traders through more inclusive financial access.
"This will enable them to grow their businesses while contributing to local economic development.  Financing alone is not enough. Entrepreneurs also need financial management knowledge, business guidance and practical digital tools.
"Agro Penjaja-i combines these elements to help build more sustainable businesses," he said.
Under Agro Penjaja-i, eligible hawkers and micro-entrepreneurs can access micro-financing of up to RM100,000.
Since its May launch, the initiative has recorded more than RM1 million in AgroSaudagar deposits across 488 accounts.
The initiative also has 370 Agrobank digital banking enrolments and 311 DuitNow QR activations.
Agrobank said DuitNow QR transactions under the programme have reached 14,248, with a total transaction value exceeding RM200,000.
"A total of 82 QR SoundBox devices have been distributed to support digital payment adoption among hawkers. On the financing front, the bank has approved 70 micro-financing accounts with a combined value exceeding RM2 million," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan raids foil attempts to smuggle cooking oil into Thailand
Article:
KOTA BARU: Smuggling syndicates have begun sourcing subsidised cooking oil from suppliers as far as Melaka and Pahang before transporting it to Kelantan for illegal trafficking across the Malaysia-Thailand border.
State Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement chief Aswadi Jaafar said the latest crackdown under Op Tiris 4.0 (Integrated) resulted in the seizure of more than 20 tonnes of subsidised cooking oil worth RM138,000 in two separate operations in Pasir Mas and Tumpat.
Aswadi said investigations showed syndicates no longer relied solely on supplies from Kelantan, but had expanded their network by obtaining subsidised cooking oil from suppliers in other states before stockpiling it near illegal jetties along the border.
"The syndicates would transport the cooking oil into Kelantan before moving it to locations near illegal jetties along the border before smuggling it into Thailand," he told a Press conference here today.
In the latest seizure, he said enforcement officers intercepted a lorry at Kampung Telaga Bata, Chabang Empat, Tumpat, at about 10.45pm on Friday.
"A search of the vehicle uncovered 1,040 boxes containing 17,680kg of subsidised cooking oil packets.
"The cooking oil was valued at RM44,200, while the total value of the seizure, including the lorry, was estimated at RM94,200," he said.
He added that a local lorry driver in his 40s and his attendant were detained to assist investigations.
"Initial investigations found that the driver was allegedly paid RM500 to deliver the load to a location determined by another individual," he added.
Earlier the same day, he said ministry enforcement officers, together with police, foiled another smuggling attempt involving two Perodua Alza multi-purpose vehicles in operations around Pasir Puteh and Pasir Mas.
"One of the vehicles was stopped near a petrol station in Jalan Pasir Puteh, where officers found 1,445kg of subsidised cooking oil of various brands.
"A 27-year-old local driver was arrested after failing to produce valid permits or documents related to the controlled goods," he said.
Aswadi said the second suspect attempted to flee by driving the vehicle into a river before crashing into a shed in Kampung Jakar, Banggol Che Dol, Pasir Mas.
"The driver escaped on foot, but officers recovered the abandoned vehicle containing another 1,360kg of subsidised cooking oil packed in 80 boxes.
"The combined seizure from both Perodua Alza vehicles was valued at RM43,000," he said.
He added that all three cases were being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961 for possessing and attempting to misappropriate subsidised controlled goods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain warning issued for KL, Selangor and nine other states until 5pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several states in the Peninsula, Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan until 5pm today.
MetMalaysia said in a statement that the areas expected to be affected in the Peninsula are Perlis; Kedah, involving Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, and Padang Terap; Perak (Manjung, Perak Tengah, and Hilir Perak); and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong, and Rompin).
Similar warnings were also issued for Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, Melaka, Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat and Muar).
In Sarawak, the areas involved are Kuching, Samarahan, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song), and Miri (Subis, Telang Usan, and Miri), while in Sabah, it affects the Interior (Kuala Penyu) and Tawau (Tawau and Semporna).
Labuan is also placed under a similar warning. — Bernama

Title: ‘Thanks for the help’: Loke says MCA’s retreat boosts DAP in three Negeri Sembilan seats
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 19 — DAP Secretary-General Anthony Loke today described MCA’s decision not to contest three of its traditional seats in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election as a move that indirectly benefits DAP by improving its chances of retaining those constituencies.
The seats in question are Lobak, Mambau, and Lukut, to give way for Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidates to contest.
“I would like to thank MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong for being so ‘generous’ in helping DAP by not contesting.
“Even if we (DAP) were to lose, we would still put up a fight, but MCA has been very generous. It is now up to MCA members to assess that decision,” he told reporters after a walkabout with six Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates at the Seremban Central Market here today.
The candidates are Ho Weng Wah (Temiang), Lee Kai Yet (Mambau), Siau Meow Kong (Rahang), Nicole Tan (Bukit Kepayang), S. Mugunthan (Seremban Jaya), and Chew Seh Yong (Lobak).
Loke, who is also Negeri Sembilan DAP chairman, claimed MCA’s decision was part of a broader political strategy aimed at enabling Barisan Nasional (BN) to secure straight fights in several constituencies through an understanding with PN.
Under the arrangement, he alleged, MCA sacrificed three of its traditional seats in exchange for PN refraining from contesting several seats held by BN.
“The objective is to avoid vote-splitting and facilitate the transfer of support,” he said.
Loke, who is also the Seremban Member of Parliament, noted that of the seven seats contested by MCA, namely Chennah, Bahau, Nilai, Temiang, Rahang, Chuah, and Repah, only Chennah will see a direct contest between DAP and MCA.
The incumbent Chennah assemblyman expressed confidence that PH, through DAP, would continue to enjoy support from voters of all ethnic backgrounds, citing his track record of serving the constituency since 2013.
In Chennah, Loke, who is Transport Minister, faces Negeri Sembilan MCA Youth chief Siow Kong Choon of BN in a straight fight.
He also expressed confidence that DAP would retain all 11 state seats it is contesting, contributing to PH’s bid to form the Negeri Sembilan state government.
“PH remains the best choice for the people of Negeri Sembilan compared to BN and PN. I therefore urge voters across the state to support PH candidates in this election,” he said.
The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 5. The Election Commission has set August 1 as the polling day, with early voting scheduled for July 28.
A total of 889,490 registered voters are eligible to cast their ballots in the state election, comprising 867,151 ordinary voters, 16,884 military personnel and their spouses, and 5,455 police personnel who will vote early.
For the latest news on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, visit [https://prn.bernama.com/n9](https://prn.bernama.com/n9). — Bernama

Title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Cousins turn political rivals in three-way contest for Klawang
Article:
JELEBU, July 19 — The campaign trail at Kuala Klawang weekend market here took an interesting turn today when two cousins contesting the Klawang state seat in the Negeri Sembilan State Election crossed paths while engaging with voters.
Incumbent Datuk Bakri Sawir of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Danni Rais of Perikatan Nasional (PN) were seen engaging with visitors and traders at the market as campaigning entered its second day today.
Their presence at almost the same time drew public attention, with some visitors taking the opportunity to shake hands, chat and take photos and videos as the two candidates passed through the same areas of the market.
Although they are from different political parties and are now vying for the same state seat, the encounter between the cousins took place in a calm and cordial atmosphere, with both continuing their campaigns separately without any provocation and even managing to exchange jokes.
Bakri, when met by Bernama, said he does not view his contest with his cousin as something extraordinary, but instead considers the election as a platform offering their services to the people.
“For me, there is no specific challenge. What’s important is that we campaign in a healthy manner and follow the rules. Yesterday, when we were reprimanded by the Jelebu District Council regarding the installation of flags on the stadium fence, we immediately took them down.
“If we do not respect the law, how can we ask others to do so?” he said.
He also reminded all parties, especially party machinery, to ensure that the campaign period proceeds in an orderly and respectful manner to preserve harmony throughout the election.
The Klawang state seat, which has 13,355 registered voters, will see a three-cornered fight involving Bakri, Danni and Bersatu’s Muhammad Adib Musa.
The Election Commission (EC) has set July 28 for early voting and August 1 for polling. For the latest updates on the state polls, visit [https://prn.bernama.com/n9](https://prn.bernama.com/n9). — Bernama

Title: Ministry: Nearly 173,000 companies register for subsidised diesel scheme nationwide, covering 465,000 vehicles
Article:
